summary: The reproduction bundle clones the vulnerable PraisonAI revision, launches the shipped Chainlit chat UI, submits the default admin/admin credentials, and verifies that Chainlit issues an authenticated admin session.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should print EVOHUNT_DEFAULT_ADMIN_LOGIN_SUCCESS after the helper authenticates with admin/admin and confirms that /user returns the admin identity from the vulnerable Chainlit app.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned commit f00763937bf7f4d091e84533692fc0576fca9b99, built the Docker image, launched the vulnerable Chainlit app, printed EVOHUNT_DEFAULT_ADMIN_LOGIN_SUCCESS, and showed /login succeeding with admin/admin before /user returned identifier=admin and metadata.role=admin.
verification_method: The helper starts the vulnerable Chainlit UI from the pinned repository revision, polls /auth/config until the service is ready, submits admin/admin to /login, then confirms that /user returns identifier=admin with credential-backed admin metadata. The run script fails closed if the success signal is absent.
evidence_produced_by_run: The container printed EVOHUNT_DEFAULT_ADMIN_LOGIN_SUCCESS after submitting admin/admin to /login.
The helper recorded /auth/config returning requireLogin=true and passwordAuth=true.
The helper recorded /user returning identifier=admin with metadata.provider=credentials and metadata.role=admin after receiving an access_token cookie.
The Chainlit app log tail included the built-in default-admin warning and a successful login message for user admin.
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction exercises the shipped ui/chat.py surface because it carries the same default admin/admin Chainlit auth pattern as the first-class realtime UI with a smaller dependency closure.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
